channel.title: ssh on kxsh
channel.description: Recent content in ssh on kxsh

item.title: Signing Git Commits
item.description: Whenever I setup a new machine, the following Git commands are a staple: git config user.name "Your Name" git config user.email "your.email@domain.ext" I have always wondered what’s preventing a person from impersonating somebody other than themselves. This curiousity led me to learning about Signatures, especially how to configure them on Git. In particular, I was interested in setting up my machine to sign via my work and personal identities. The solution to the generic problem of identification (authentication) is addressed with the concept of “digital signature”.
